pattern: Layered dynamic programming counts, for each remaining move budget, how many ways the ball reaches each cell, accumulating exits when a step would cross the boundary, all under a modulus.
hints: Think of how many distinct paths land on each cell after exactly k moves, starting from a single cell at zero moves.
Each move spreads a cell's path count to its four neighbours; a neighbour outside the grid contributes directly to the exit total.
Iterate the move budget upward, and for every interior cell add its current count to the four neighbours of the next layer, counting boundary crossings as exits, all modulo 1e9+7.

edge_inputs.description: corner start in a small grid
zero moves means no path can exit
single cell grid exits immediately on first move in four directions
interior start far from boundary needs several moves
large grid and large move budget exercises modulo

stress.description: random valid configuration within constraint bounds
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = rng.randrange(1, 51)
    n = rng.randrange(1, 51)
    mv = rng.randrange(0, 51)
    sr = rng.randrange(0, m)
    sc = rng.randrange(0, n)
    return [m, n, mv, sr, sc]
stress.note: start cell always inside the grid
